info.name: Create a pending order for certificate

items.docs: Creating a certificate order can be a long running asynchronous operation in the PKI workflow. The PKI API supports 2 options for getting the completion stateful actions for this asynchronous operations: 1) by polling operations -- see /v1/certificates/{certificateId}/actions 2) via WebHook style callback -- see '/v1/certificates/{certificateId}/callback'.

info.name: Validate a pending order for certificate

items.docs: Validate a pending order for certificate

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to lookup

items.docs: Once the certificate order has been created, this method can be used to check the status of the certificate. This method can also be used to retrieve details of the certificate.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to register for callback

items.docs: This method is used to retrieve all stateful actions relating to a certificate lifecycle.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to resend email

params.name: emailId
params.description: Email id for email to resend

items.docs: This method can be used to resend emails by providing the certificate id and the email id

params.description: Certificate id to resend emails

params.name: emailAddress
params.description: Specific email address to resend email

items.docs: This method adds an alternate email address to a certificate order and re-sends all existing request emails to that address.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to resend emails

params.name: emailId
params.description: Email id for email to resend

params.name: emailAddress

items.docs: This method can be used to resend emails by providing the certificate id, the email id, and the recipient email address

items.docs: This method can be used to retrieve all emails sent for a certificate.

info.name: Retrieve system stateful action callback url

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to register for stateful action callback

items.docs: This method is used to retrieve the registered callback url for a certificate.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to register/replace for callback

params.name: callbackUrl
params.description: Callback url registered/replaced to receive stateful actions

items.docs: This method is used to register/replace url for callbacks for stateful actions relating to a certificate lifecycle. The callback url is a Webhook style pattern and will receive POST http requests with json body defined in the CertificateAction model definition for each certificate action.  Only one callback URL is allowed to be registered for each certificateId, so it will replace a previous registration.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to unregister callback

items.docs: Unregister the callback for a particular certificate.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to cancel

items.docs: Use the cancel call to cancel a pending certificate order.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to download

items.docs: Download certificate

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to reissue

items.docs: Rekeying is the process by which the private and public key is changed for a certificate. It is a simplified reissue,where only the CSR is changed. Reissuing is the process by which domain names are added or removed from a certificate.Once a request is validated and approved, the certificate will be reissued with the new common name and sans specified. Unlimited reissues are available during the lifetime of the certificate.New names added to a certificate that do not share the base domain of

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to renew

items.docs: Renewal is the process by which the validity of a certificate is extended. Renewal is only available 60 days prior to expiration of the previous certificate and 30 days after the expiration of the previous certificate. The renewal supports modifying a set of the original certificate order information. Once a request is validated and approved, the certificate will be issued with extended validity. Since subject alternative names can be removed during a renewal, we require that you provide the sub

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to revoke

items.docs: Use revoke call to revoke an active certificate, if the certificate has not been issued a 404 response will be returned.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id

params.name: theme
params.description: This value represents the visual theme of the seal. If seal doesn't exist, default values are used if params not present. If seal does exist, default values will not be used to update unless params present.

params.name: locale
params.description: Determine locale for text displayed in seal image and verification page. If seal doesn't exist, default values are used if params not present. If seal does exist, default values will not be used to update unless params present.

items.docs: This method is used to obtain the site seal information for an issued certificate. A site seal is a graphic that the certificate purchaser can embed on their web site to show their visitors information about their SSL certificate. If a web site visitor clicks on the site seal image, a pop-up page is displayed that contains detailed information about the SSL certificate. The site seal token is used to link the site seal graphic image to the appropriate certificate details pop-up page display w

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to lookup

items.docs: Domain control is a means for verifying the domain included in the certificate order. This resource is useful for resellers that control the domains for their customers, and can expedite the verification process. See https://www.godaddy.com/help/verifying-your-domain-ownership-for-ssl-certificate-requests-html-or-dns-7452

params.name: entitlementId
params.description: Entitlement id to lookup

params.name: latest
params.description: Fetch only the most recent certificate

items.docs: Once the certificate order has been created, this method can be used to check the status of the certificate. This method can also be used to retrieve details of the certificates associated to an entitlement.

info.name: Create a pending order for certificate

items.docs: Creating a certificate order for a subscription can be a long running asynchronous operation in the PKI workflow. The PKI API supports 2 options for getting the completion stateful actions for this asynchronous operations: 1) by polling operations -- see /v1/certificates/{certificateId}/actions 2) via WebHook style callback -- see '/v1/certificates/{certificateId}/callback'.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to reissue

items.docs: Rekeying is the process by which the private and public key is changed for a certificate. It is a simplified reissue,where only the CSR is changed. Reissue extends validity of the existing certificate by requesting a new certificate with all the same values as existing issued certificate. Once a request is validated and approved, the certificate will be reissued with the same common name and sans specified from existing certificate. Unlimited reissues are available during the lifetime of the

params.description: Entitlement id to download

params.description: An identifier for a customer

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return

items.docs:
This method can be used to retrieve a list of certificates for a specified customer.
  • **shopperId** is **not the same** as **customerId**. **shopperId** is a number of max length 10 digits (*ex:* 1234567890) whereas **customerId** is a UUIDv4 (*ex:* 295e3bc3-b3b9-4d95-aae5-ede41a994d13)

info.name: Retrieve detailed information for supplied domain

params.name: customerId
params.description: An identifier for a customer

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Certificate id to lookup

params.name: domain
params.description: A valid domain name in the certificate request

items.docs:
Retrieve detailed information for supplied domain, including domain verification details and Certificate Authority Authorization (CAA) verification details.
  • **shopperId** is **not the same** as **customerId**. **shopperId** is a number of max length 10 digits (*ex:* 1234567890) whereas **customerId** is a UUIDv4 (*ex:* 295e3bc3-b3b9-4d95-aae5-ede41a994d13)

info.name: Retrieves the external account binding for the specified customer

params.name: customerId
params.description: An identifier for a customer

items.docs: Use this endpoint to retrieve a key identifier and Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) key for Automated Certificate Management Environment (ACME) External Account Binding (EAB). These credentials can be used with an ACME client that supports EAB (ex. CertBot) to automate the issuance request and deployment of DV SSL certificates

info.name: Get a page of subscriptions by domain

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of records to return per query.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number. (First page is one instead of zero.)

params.name: domain
params.description:
Filter by domain name / common name.
This will look for the text inside the content of the domain.
This is not a "find domains that start with x" search.

params.name: status
params.description:
The certificate lifecycle stage.
Active, Expired, and Revoked are all Issued certificates.
Active means the validity period includes today.
Expired means the validity period was prior to today.
Revoked means that the certificate has been revoked.
Pending means that the certificate is still going through the issuance process and has not been signed or issued yet.
Denied means that the certificate went from pending to denied for any valid denial reason. (Customer canceled request, CAA records exist for the domain, fraud, etc...)

params.name: type
params.description: The type of certificate in terms of the number of applicable domains.

params.name: validation
params.description:
The validation type for the certificate. 
Standard/Basic Validation (DV). 
Organizational/Deluxe Vetting (OV).
Extended/Premium Validation (EV).

items.docs: The pagination starts at page 1. Each page contains a page of *subscriptions*, not certificates. This endpoint is meant for paging the subscriptions under the authorized user's account. Each subscription contains a snapshot of certificates contained within the subscription. To fetch further certificates under a subscription, use the /v2/certificates/subscription/{guid} endpoint with the subscription GUID obtained from this call. If any filtering is applied, subscriptions without any certificates
